name: DeepFlow Research Assistant
description: Example flow demonstrating DeepFlow integration. Routes user messages through a DeepFlow Agent component that calls the Deep Agents runtime for multi-step research tasks with planning, sub-agents, and filesystem context.

node.description: Receives user messages from the playground
Sends tasks to the DeepFlow runtime for Deep Agents execution. The runtime runs in an isolated environment with LangChain 1.x and the full Deep Agents SDK.

runtime_url.info: URL of the DeepFlow runtime server. Use http://deepflow-runtime:8100 in Docker, or http://localhost:8100 for manual setup.

system_prompt.value: You are a research assistant. Use your planning tools to break down complex research questions. Write findings to your filesystem for context management. Spawn sub-agents for parallel research when appropriate.
system_prompt.info: System prompt passed to the Deep Agent

timeout.info: Maximum execution time before the request times out

input_value.info: The message/task to send to the Deep Agent

node.description: Displays agent response in the playground

text.value:
This flow demonstrates the DeepFlow bridge pattern:

1. User message enters via Chat Input
2. DeepFlow Component sends it over HTTP to the DeepFlow Runtime
3. The Runtime runs a Deep Agent with planning, sub-agents, and filesystem tools
4. The agent's response returns through the bridge to Chat Output

The Runtime runs in a separate Python environment with LangChain 1.x and Deep Agents installed. This solves the dependency conflict with Langflow's LangChain 0.3.x requirement.

For Docker: runtime_url = http://deepflow-runtime:8100
For manual setup: runtime_url = http://localhost:8100
